interactions.summary: Kim asks what chance there is that none of Jiang's war forecast happens. Jiang answers that the Rubicon has been crossed, there is no return to 2020 normal, and even Democratic midterm gains would likely push more war through national-security candidates.

Kim summarizes the war map and suggests U.S. entry into Iran would require Israel to start losing, while Jiang adds that 2026 will likely include false flags against U.S. interests or even the homeland.

Jiang says China will finance and develop but not send troops, and reads Trump's national security strategy not as retreat into spheres of influence but as a harder divide-and-rule imperial strategy that abandons liberal multilateral language for pure power politics.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_01 is Kim pressing consequences; SPEAKER_00 is Jiang, including brief agreement at seg-0110.

claims.claim: Jiang says the world has crossed the Rubicon and cannot return to the pre-2020 normal; Democrats winning midterms would not stop escalation because their candidates would come from the national security apparatus and push wars overseas.
Jiang predicts false flags in 2026 against American interests in Iraq, Syria, possibly the homeland, as part of the path to war with Iran.
Jiang says China will participate through development and financing, but will not send troops anywhere.

Jiang rejects the reading that Trump's national security strategy means U.S. retreat into spheres of influence; instead, he says America will maintain empire by abandoning liberal multilateral institutions and using divide-and-rule power politics.

Jiang says the United States will not leave East Asia, Europe, or Africa; it will promote Japan against China, overthrow liberal European regimes in favor of Trump-aligned nations, and fight China for influence and economic dominance in Africa.

signature_moments.moment: The question of avoiding world war is answered with a classical threshold: the Rubicon has already been crossed.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives Jiang's forecast its irreversibility claim.

signature_moments.moment: Trump's empire drops the rules-based mask and says the quiet part out loud.
signature_moments.source_phrase: screw that we're an empire might is right
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase crystallizes Jiang's distinction between liberal empire and naked empire.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The imperial strategy Jiang says the United States will use to maintain control after abandoning liberal multilateral organizations.
The older American imperial language Jiang says Trump discards in favor of explicit might-is-right empire.
An attack Jiang expects could be used to mobilize American support for Iran war.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-01 Jiang says there is no return to 2020 normal and predicts 2026 false flags as part of Iran-war escalation.

uncertainty_notes.note: The phrase 'creating spirits of influence' is likely ASR for 'spheres of influence' and is treated that way semantically.
